title: Free Theary Seng: The US Should Not Forget About Cambodia’s Political Prisoners
gist: American lawyer and rights activist Theary Seng remains in a Cambodian prison after a 2022 six-year sentence for 'treason' over Facebook posts criticizing Hun Sen, exemplifying Cambodia's authoritarian crackdowns; the authors urge the US and international community to act. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: American lawyer and human rights activist Theary Seng was sentenced to six years in Cambodian prison for 'conspiracy to commit treason' and 'incitement of social disorder' over Facebook posts criticizing strongman Hun Sen, in a mass trial alongside 60 opposition members.
insights.speaker: Jared Genser and Hannah Pierce-Carty
insights.so_what: Jailing a US-citizen activist over social-media speech illustrates Cambodia's rule-of-law and political-risk environment for foreigners and investors.
insights.quote: Theary Seng was sentenced to six years in Cambodian prison for “conspiracy to commit treason” and “incitement of social disorder.” Her crime? Facebook posts criticizing Hun Sen

insights.claim: Theary's case is emblematic of Cambodia's broader authoritarian reality of crackdowns on activists, shuttered independent media, and sham elections.
insights.speaker: Jared Genser and Hannah Pierce-Carty
insights.so_what: Entrenched authoritarian governance signals limited institutional checks and elevated governance risk in Cambodia.
insights.quote: Amid crackdowns on activists, shuttered independent media, and sham elections
